'Removing one changes the construct' matches 'excluding an indicator changes the composition of the latent variable' (p.308) and 'Omitting an indicator is omitting a part of the construct' (p.309). faithfulness.notes: Independent A-I pass confirms faithful single-source usage at all four sites; no quantifier overreach, no omitted internal caveat that distorts the cited point.

best_source_flags: None. faithfulness.direction: Correct. Both sites use the citation only to describe how the EKC constructs reference eigenvalues; the paper's own account (first reference = lup = (1+sqrt(gamma))^2, the null-model asymptotic maximum, plus a serial proportional correction by remaining variance) matches the manuscript's description in both direction and content.
faithfulness.magnitude: No numbers from Braeken & van Assen are quoted in the manuscript (all reported factor counts/eigenvalues are the study's own \val macros), so there is no numeric claim to mis-state.
faithfulness.operationalization: Accurate. 'Reference eigenvalues start at the null-model maximum' = lEKC_1 = lup = (1+sqrt(gamma))^2 (p.452). 'Serial correction for the variance preceding factors have already absorbed' = the (J - sum l_j)/(J-j+1) correction factor, the average remaining variance after accounting for the first (j-1) observed eigenvalues (p.454).
faithfulness.population: N/A in the usual sense; EKC is a factor-retention statistic. The 'embedding dimension in the sample-size role' is the manuscript's own domain adaptation of gamma = J/n, presented as the authors' adaptation rather than as Braeken's claim.
faithfulness.attribution_layer: Correct. The citation supports the EKC method description; the embedding-specific adaptation (embedding dimension as n, similarity matrix of items) is framed as the manuscript's own contribution, not attributed to Braeken & van Assen.

best_source_flags: This is the primary source that originated the EKC (Braeken & van Assen, 2017, Psychological Methods). No better source exists for the method; citation is correct and non-substitutable.

R1 was over-cautious here. All other R1 sub-checks (direction, 463-pairs magnitude, operationalization, population, attribution, precedence, grouping) I independently confirm as correct.
